title: Getting Started with htna
text: Start loading package example data shipped Nestimate: build_htna() accepts named list long-format data frames, one per actor group. combines sequences, estimates transition probabilities, stores actor partition resulting network object: actor partition available two forms: net$nodes$groups – actor label per node (auto-detected plot_htna()). net$node_groups – canonical (node, group) data frame used cograph::get_groups() cograph::cluster_summary().

title: Getting Started with htna
text: plot_htna() (re-exported cograph) auto-detects actor groups renders distinct colours:

title: Getting Started with htna
text: Meta-paths type-level patterns abstract away concrete states ask often typed template appears data. example, Human->AI->Human captures every length-3 path starts Human code, transitions AI code, returns Human code. can search specific schema (wildcards): Gapped meta-paths allow skipping positions: Filter lift find -represented meta-paths:
code: # Enumerate all meta-paths of length 2 to 4 mp <- extract_meta_paths(net) mp #> Meta-paths (type-level) over 429 sequences #> Rows: 28 | Lengths: 2, 3, 4 | Gaps: 0 #>            schema length gap count n_seq support frequency lift #>         Human->AI      2   0  5970   428   0.998     0.316 1.28 #>         AI->Human      2   0  5693   424   0.988     0.301 1.22 #>      Human->Human      2   0  4674   422   0.984     0.247 0.79 #>            AI->AI      2   0  2581   403   0.939     0.136 0.70 #>  Human->AI->Human      3   0  3593   402   0.937     0.194 1.41 #>  Human->Human->AI      3   0  3172   422   0.984     0.172 1.25 #>  AI->Human->Human      3   0  2828   403   0.939     0.153 1.11 #>     AI->Human->AI      3   0  2744   383   0.893     0.148 1.36 #>     Human->AI->AI      3   0  2189   403   0.939     0.118 1.09 #>     AI->AI->Human      3   0  2100   397   0.925     0.114 1.04 #> ... (18 more) # Any length-3 path that returns to Human extract_meta_paths(net, schema = "Human->*->Human") #> Meta-paths (type-level) over 429 sequences #> Rows: 2 | Lengths: 3 | Gaps: 0 #>               schema length gap count n_seq support frequency lift #>     Human->AI->Human      3   0  3593   402   0.937     0.709 1.41 #>  Human->Human->Human      3   0  1472   396   0.923     0.291 0.46 extract_meta_paths(net, length = 3, type = "gapped", gap = 1:2) #> Meta-paths (type-level) over 429 sequences #> Rows: 16 | Lengths: 3 | Gaps: 1, 2 #>               schema length gap count n_seq support frequency lift #>     Human->AI->Human      3   1  3265   408   0.951     0.185 1.35 #>     Human->Human->AI      3   1  2722   413   0.963     0.154 1.12 #>        AI->Human->AI      3   1  2515   379   0.883     0.143 1.31 #>     AI->Human->Human      3   1  2242   391   0.911     0.127 0.92 #>  Human->Human->Human      3   1  2146   372   0.867     0.122 0.70 #>        Human->AI->AI      3   1  1748   384   0.895     0.099 0.91 #>        AI->AI->Human      3   1  1618   360   0.839     0.092 0.84 #>           AI->AI->AI      3   1  1382   304   0.709     0.078 0.91 #>  Human->Human->Human      3   2  2948   399   0.930     0.176 1.01 #>     Human->Human->AI      3   2  2341   390   0.909     0.139 1.01 #> ... (6 more) extract_meta_paths(net, length = 3, min_lift = 1.2) #> Meta-paths (type-level) over 429 sequences #> Rows: 3 | Lengths: 3 | Gaps: 0 #>            schema length gap count n_seq support frequency lift #>  Human->AI->Human      3   0  3593   402   0.937     0.194 1.41 #>  Human->Human->AI      3   0  3172   422   0.984     0.172 1.25 #>     AI->Human->AI      3   0  2744   383   0.893     0.148 1.36

what: Build a Heterogeneous Transition Network (HTNA) — build_htna
title: Build a Heterogeneous Transition Network (HTNA) — build_htna
text: Builds transition network combined sequence two actor groups (e.g. Human AI) preserves actor partition result downstream plotting analysis can treat actor's codes distinct node group.

title: Build a Heterogeneous Transition Network (HTNA) — build_htna
code: build_htna(   data,   actor_col = NULL,   action = "code",   session = "session_id",   order = "order_in_session",   method = "relative",   disambiguate = FALSE,   ... )

title: Build a Heterogeneous Transition Network (HTNA) — build_htna
text: data Either: named list long-format data frames, one per actor (e.g. list(Human = human_long, AI = ai_long)). frames must share column schema. single long-format data frame actor column. case actor_col must supplied. actor_col Character. Name actor column data single data frame. Ignored data named list. action Character. Name action/code column. Default "code". session Character. Name session column. Default "session_id". order Character. Name within-session order column. Default "order_in_session". method Character. Transition method passed build_network: "relative" (default), "frequency", "attention". disambiguate Logical. FALSE (default), function errors code label appears one actor group. TRUE, codes prefixed actor name ("Human:Ask", "AI:Ask") become distinct nodes. ... Additional arguments forwarded build_network.

title: Build a Heterogeneous Transition Network (HTNA) — build_htna
text: netobject actor partition stored cograph's canonical schema, cograph::plot_htna(net) auto-detects groups arguments: $nodes$groups - actor label per node (column name cograph::plot_htna auto-detects). $node_groups - data frame columns node group (canonical cograph_network schema, also readable cograph::get_groups, cluster_summary, print method cograph_network). slots exactly returned build_network.

title: Build a Heterogeneous Transition Network (HTNA) — build_htna
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ data(human_long, ai_long, package = "Nestimate") net <- build_htna(list(Human = human_long, AI = ai_long)) net$node_groups            # canonical (node, group) schema cograph::plot_htna(net)    # auto-detects $nodes$groups, no other args } # }

what: Extract Meta-Paths from a Heterogeneous Transition Network — extract_meta_paths
title: Extract Meta-Paths from a Heterogeneous Transition Network — extract_meta_paths
text: Discovers type-level patterns (meta-paths) actor-typed sequences produced heterogeneous transition network. meta-path fixed sequence node types - example Human->AI->Human - abstracts away concrete states asks often typed template appears data. Two regimes supported: enumerate every meta-path given lengths (default), search one specific schema (optional wildcards).

title: Extract Meta-Paths from a Heterogeneous Transition Network — extract_meta_paths
code: extract_meta_paths(   x,   length = 2:4,   schema = NULL,   type = c("contiguous", "gapped"),   gap = 1L,   min_count = 1L,   min_support = 0,   min_lift = 0,   start = NULL,   end = NULL,   contain = NULL )

title: Extract Meta-Paths from a Heterogeneous Transition Network — extract_meta_paths
text: x [htna_network] network built build_htna(). Must $nodes$groups, $node_groups, $data populated. length [integer(): 2:4] Meta-path lengths enumerate. Ignored schema supplied. schema [character(1)] Optional. meta-path template search , written actor names separated "->" (e.g. "Human->AI->Human"). Wildcards supported: "Human->*->Human" matches every length-3 meta-path starts ends Human, type . type [character(1): "contiguous"]"contiguous" (default) considers consecutive positions; "gapped" considers positions spaced gap + 1 apart. gap [integer(): 1L] Gap size(s) used type = "gapped". Multiple values produce one row per (length, gap) combination, distinguished gap column. min_count [integer(1): 1L] Minimum total occurrences retain. min_support [numeric(1): 0] Minimum proportion sequences containing meta-path least . min_lift [numeric(1): 0] Minimum lift (observed / expected marginal independence). 0 disables filter. start [character()] Keep meta-paths whose first type set. end [character()] Keep meta-paths whose last type set. contain [character()] Keep meta-paths whose type sequence contains elements set (order).

title: Extract Meta-Paths from a Heterogeneous Transition Network — extract_meta_paths
text: object class c("htna_meta_paths", "htna_paths", "data.frame") columns: schema, length, gap, count, n_sequences, support, frequency, lift. Attributes: n_sequences, alphabet, level = "meta".

title: Extract Meta-Paths from a Heterogeneous Transition Network — extract_meta_paths
text: Operates actor partition stored network build_htna().

title: Extract Meta-Paths from a Heterogeneous Transition Network — extract_meta_paths
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ data(human_long, ai_long, package = "Nestimate") net <- build_htna(list(Human = human_long, AI = ai_long))  # All meta-paths of length 2..4 extract_meta_paths(net)  # Wildcard family search: any length-3 path that returns to Human extract_meta_paths(net, schema = "Human->*->Human")  # Gapped meta-paths extract_meta_paths(net, length = 3, type = "gapped", gap = 1:2)  # Lift threshold (over-represented relative to type marginals) extract_meta_paths(net, length = 3, min_lift = 1.2) } # }

what: htna: Heterogeneous Transition Network Analysis — htna-package
title: htna: Heterogeneous Transition Network Analysis — htna-package
text: Build, analyze, visualize transition networks sequences mix two actor groups (e.g. Human AI) within interaction.

title: htna: Heterogeneous Transition Network Analysis — htna-package
text: package thin layer top two foundations: Nestimate - estimation, bootstrap, permutation, reliability, centrality, Markov machinery. cograph - rendering (splot, plot_htna, transitions, communities, chord, heatmap). htna adds heterogeneous-actor wrapper build_htna re-exports commonly used downstream functions users need attach Nestimate cograph explicitly.

title: htna: Heterogeneous Transition Network Analysis — htna-package
